prerequisites: A TMDB account. Sign up at themoviedb.org, since an API key can only be requested from a logged-in account.

An approved TMDB API key, requested from the API section of your account settings after agreeing to the terms of use. TMDB notes the registration process is not optimized for mobile, so use a desktop browser.
A language/locale code for the language config field (for example en-US) used to localize TMDB responses.
A movie_id value, the numeric TMDB identifier of the movie you want to fetch.
A query value, the search term used by TMDB search endpoints.

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: TMDB application-level auth. Provide the v3 API key in the api_key secret field. TMDB passes this key as a query-string parameter named api_key (for example ?api_key=YOUR_KEY). The key is generated from the API section of your TMDB account settings.

setupSteps.title: Create a TMDB account
setupSteps.body: Sign up for a free account at themoviedb.org. An account is required because the API key can only be requested while logged in.

setupSteps.title: Request an API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to TMDB, open your account settings, go to the API section, and request an API key. Agree to the terms of use to have the key issued. Use a desktop browser, since TMDB notes the API registration pages are not optimized for mobile.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Enter your v3 API key in the required api_key field. pm authenticates by sending it as the api_key query parameter on TMDB requests.

setupSteps.title: Configure request fields
setupSteps.body: Set the required language field to a locale such as en-US, set movie_id to the numeric TMDB movie identifier you want to read, and set query to the search term used by TMDB search endpoints.

sources.title: TMDB Docs: Application authentication (API key and Read Access Token)
